import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load motion data
data = np.loadtxt('/app/motion_data.txt', delimiter=',', skiprows=1)
frames = data[:, 0]
motion_raw = data[:, 1]
motion_smooth = data[:, 2]

# Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.plot(frames, motion_raw, 'b-', alpha=0.5, label='Raw motion')
plt.plot(frames, motion_smooth, 'g-', linewidth=2, label='Smoothed motion')

# Mark candidate frames
candidates = [(38, 82), (107, 118)]
colors = ['red', 'purple']
labels = ['Algorithm 2', 'Algorithm 1']

for (takeoff, landing), color, label in zip(candidates, colors, labels):
    plt.axvline(x=takeoff, color=color, linestyle='--', alpha=0.7, label=f'{label} takeoff ({takeoff})')
    plt.axvline(x=landing, color=color, linestyle=':', alpha=0.7, label=f'{label} landing ({landing})')

plt.xlabel('Frame Number')
plt.ylabel('Motion Percentage')
plt.title('Motion Analysis with Candidate Jump Frames')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.savefig('/app/motion_with_candidates.png', dpi=150)
print("Saved plot to /app/motion_with_candidates.png")

# Print motion values at candidate frames
print("\nMotion values at candidate frames:")
print("Frame | Raw Motion | Smoothed Motion")
print("-" * 40)
for frame in [38, 82, 107, 118]:

    if len(idx) > 0:
        idx = idx[0]
        print(f"{frame:5d} | {motion_raw[idx]:10.6f} | {motion_smooth[idx]:10.6f}")
